content.content:
- content: 'Ticket-holder entry notice for Northstar Live 2026 at Wembley Stadium
    on

    12 July 2026.


    For this event, standard personal bags will not be admitted, regardless of

    whether they fit the venue''s general size allowance. Attendees should not

    rely on the ordinary personal-bag allowance for this event.


    Medical and accessibility-related carriage is handled separately under

    Wembley Stadium''s formal medical or accessibility exception process. This

    notice does not determine whether a particular attendee qualifies for that

    process and does not replace the separate exception requirements.

    '

content.content:
content: "# Web Document

## Metadata

- title: Restricted Bag Policy - Wembley\
  \ Stadium
- url: https://www.wembleystadium.com/plan-your-visit/stadium-guide/restricted-bag-policy
\
  - source: Wembley Stadium
- domain: wembleystadium.com

## Content

# Bag policy
\
  
Wembley Stadium restricts what visitors may bring into the venue in order to support\
  \ safety and speed up security screening.

## What is allowed?

Each person\
  \ may bring one small bag. The bag must not be larger than A4 size:

- Height:\
  \ 297 mm (11.7 inches)
- Width: 210 mm (8.27 inches)
- Depth: 210 mm (8.27 inches)
\
  
The measurement applies to the overall physical size of the bag. A bag that is\
  \ only partly filled, folded, or compressed to appear smaller does not qualify on\
  \ that basis. Visitors carrying bags that do not meet the criteria will not be admitted\
  \ with those bags.

The stadium explains that limiting bag size supports public\
  \ safety, improves security around the venue, and helps the screening process move\
  \ more quickly.

## Official merchandise

Matchday merchandise purchased inside\
  \ the event operation is supplied in a clear plastic bag and may be brought in.\
  \ Merchandise bought from an external stadium retail outlet is placed in a sealed\
  \ bag. The seal should remain intact until the visitor has entered the stadium.
\
  
## Security inspection

All items and bags carried by spectators and staff are\
  \ inspected when entering. Screening may include electronic wanding or a pat-down.
\
  
Bags are checked at an outer security cordon. When there is a dispute about size,\
  \ staff may use a bag gauge.

## Medical exceptions

Wembley makes exceptions\
  \ for medical equipment and some other medically necessary items, but permission\
  \ must be arranged in advance. Visitors who need this should contact the stadium\
  \ before the event and select “Medical Exemption Certificate” as the topic.
\
  
An eligible visitor receives a certificate showing that specified items are permitted.\
  \ The certificate must be shown to security. The bag is still searched and may also\
  \ be subject to an X-ray scan before entry.

## Phones, cameras, tablets, and\
  \ handbags

A phone or camera may be carried separately, but a visitor may still\
  \ bring only one bag in total under the policy.

Handbags count as the one small\
  \ permitted bag. A handbag may be brought in when it remains within the A4-size\
  \ limit and meets the rest of the bag policy.

Wembley notes that its restricted\
  \ bag policy is different from the NFL Clear Bag Policy.
"
metadata: {}
title: Restricted Bag Policy - Wembley Stadium
url: https://www.wembleystadium.com/plan-your-visit/stadium-guide/restricted-bag-policy

content: '# Web Document


  ## Metadata


  - title: Medical Exemption Certificates - Wembley Support Portal

  - url: https://help.wembleystadium.com/support/solutions/articles/7000096222-medical-exemption-certificates

  - source: Wembley Support Portal

  - domain: help.wembleystadium.com


  ## Content


  # Medical Exemption Certificates


  Wembley Stadium can issue a Medical Exemption Certificate when a visitor needs a
  bag that does not comply with the ordinary bag policy or needs to bring items that
  are not usually permitted, such as food or drink, for medical reasons.


  The certificate must be presented to security when entering. A certificate does
  not remove security screening: the bag is still searched on arrival.


  ## When a certificate is not required


  Visitors should first review the prohibited-items guidance. If the item, or the
  material it is made from, is allowed and can fit inside a standard A4 bag, a Medical
  Exemption Certificate is not required.



  ## Supporting documentation


  Supporting documentation is required before Wembley can issue a Medical Exemption
  Certificate. The request cannot be processed without it.


  Supporting evidence may include a letter from a hospital specialist, GP, clinical
  nurse, or an equivalent medical professional.


  ## Information required for a request


  A request should include:


  - Full name

  - Event

  - Date of event

  - Phone number

  - Ticket details, including turnstile, block number, and seat number

  - Details of what is required, such as food and drink, medical equipment, a mobility
  aid, medication, or another item

  - Whether a bag larger than the ordinary bag-policy limit is required

  - Confirmation that supporting documentation has been attached


  ## Water and event-specific variation


  Visitors bringing water may only bring a clear, empty, crushable plastic bottle
  of 500 ml or less. This rule also applies to guests with a Medical Exemption Certificate.


  The water rule may vary by event, so visitors should check the information for the
  event they are attending. For concerts, water bottles are not permitted in pitch-standing
  areas; water brought into the stadium is decanted into a plastic cup before entry
  to the pitch.


  ## Timing


  Visitors should request a Medical Exemption Certificate only when their event is
  within the next seven days. Wembley states that certificates cannot be issued earlier
  than that timeframe.

  '
metadata: {}
title: Medical Exemption Certificates - Wembley Support Portal
url: https://help.wembleystadium.com/support/solutions/articles/7000096222-medical-exemption-certificates

content: '# Web Document


  ## Metadata


  - title: Bag Policy - Wembley Support Portal

  - url: https://help.wembleystadium.com/support/solutions/articles/7000028442-bag-policy

  - source: Wembley Support Portal

  - domain: help.wembleystadium.com


  ## Content


  # Bag Policy


  Visitors should review the applicable bag rules before travelling to Wembley Stadium.


  ## Event-owner policies


  Different event owners can have their own bag policies. Wembley specifically tells
  visitors attending events such as UEFA, NFL, and Rugby fixtures to check the specific
  event page for the details that apply to that event.



  ## Entry and screening


  Ticket holders may not bring a bag into the stadium unless it complies with the
  Wembley regulations applicable to the visit. Entry with a non-compliant bag will
  be refused.


  Bags are checked at an outer security cordon. If staff need to resolve a disagreement
  about size, a bag gauge may be used.


  Items and bags carried by spectators and staff are inspected on entry. Screening
  may include electronic wanding or a pat-down.


  ## Merchandise bags


  Matchday merchandise is supplied in a clear plastic bag that is admissible under
  the policy. Merchandise purchased from an external stadium concession or store may
  be placed in a sealed bag because the purchase takes place outside the security
  cordon. Visitors should keep that seal intact until after entering the venue.


  ## Medical cases


  A medical need involving a bag or medication that does not comply with the ordinary
  policy is handled through the separate Medical Exemption Certificate process before
  the event. A certificate issued through that process must be shown at the outer
  security cordon, and the bag remains subject to search and possible X-ray screening.

  '

title: Bag Policy - Wembley Support Portal
url: https://help.wembleystadium.com/support/solutions/articles/7000028442-bag-policy

Your event is **Northstar Live 2026 at Wembley Stadium on 12 July 2026**.

### Can you bring the bag?

- Your bag measures **280 × 200 × 180 mm**, so it fits Wembley’s usual A4-size limit of **297 × 210 × 210 mm**.
- However, the event-specific notice says **standard personal bags will not be admitted for this event**, even if they meet the ordinary size limit.
- Your bag contains **prescribed medication and compact monitoring equipment**, so it may qualify for Wembley’s **medical exception process**. You should **not rely on the normal bag allowance**.

You have supporting documentation ready, but your records do not show that a **Medical Exemption Certificate** has been issued. You should contact Wembley before the event and request one, specifying the medication, monitoring equipment, bag dimensions, event details, and attaching your documentation. The event is within Wembley’s stated seven-day request window.

Even with a certificate, the bag will be searched and may be X-rayed.

### Entry process

Your current assignment is:

1. Go to the **outer cordon bag-check area**.
2. Present your **Medical Exemption Certificate** to security, if issued.
3. Have the bag inspected; additional screening such as wanding, pat-down, or X-ray may apply.
4. You have active **Fast Entry Plus**, so you may use the **priority entry queue** and receive expedited queue processing once the applicable screening requirements are met.

Fast Entry Plus **does not waive or change the event’s bag policy** and cannot substitute for a medical certificate.

Sources: [Wembley Restricted Bag Policy](https://www.wembleystadium.com/plan-your-visit/stadium-guide/restricted-bag-policy) and [Medical Exemption Certificates](https://help.wembleystadium.com/support/solutions/articles/7000096222-medical-exemption-certificates).

final_answer:
Your event is **Northstar Live 2026 at Wembley Stadium on 12 July 2026**.

### Can you bring the bag?

- Your bag measures **280 × 200 × 180 mm**, so it fits Wembley’s usual A4-size limit of **297 × 210 × 210 mm**.
- However, the event-specific notice says **standard personal bags will not be admitted for this event**, even if they meet the ordinary size limit.
- Your bag contains **prescribed medication and compact monitoring equipment**, so it may qualify for Wembley’s **medical exception process**. You should **not rely on the normal bag allowance**.

You have supporting documentation ready, but your records do not show that a **Medical Exemption Certificate** has been issued. You should contact Wembley before the event and request one, specifying the medication, monitoring equipment, bag dimensions, event details, and attaching your documentation. The event is within Wembley’s stated seven-day request window.

Even with a certificate, the bag will be searched and may be X-rayed.

### Entry process

Your current assignment is:

1. Go to the **outer cordon bag-check area**.
2. Present your **Medical Exemption Certificate** to security, if issued.
3. Have the bag inspected; additional screening such as wanding, pat-down, or X-ray may apply.
4. You have active **Fast Entry Plus**, so you may use the **priority entry queue** and receive expedited queue processing once the applicable screening requirements are met.

Fast Entry Plus **does not waive or change the event’s bag policy** and cannot substitute for a medical certificate.

Sources: [Wembley Restricted Bag Policy](https://www.wembleystadium.com/plan-your-visit/stadium-guide/restricted-bag-policy) and [Medical Exemption Certificates](https://help.wembleystadium.com/support/solutions/articles/7000096222-medical-exemption-certificates).
